name: flap-vault-ui-template-agent-contract

uiPatternReference.rule: Read before implementing Component.tsx. Use snippets as sanitized layout and state-flow references only; do not copy private Flap source code, private names, addresses, endpoints, or constants.

uiImplementationChecklist.actionVisibilityRule: Do not silently hide supported actions. If an action is stage-gated or unavailable, render the section with a clear disabled/unavailable state.

hostRuntime.tokenMetadataPreviewCapability: The template preview shell first asks the same-origin host proxy for token presentation data, then falls back to public SDK ERC20 symbol() and name() reads from the preview tokenAddress. The mocked /logo.png image is reserved for the neutral preview fixture unless tokenImageUrl is explicitly overridden.
shellOwnedUi: token breadcrumb, image, symbol/name, CA, copy, explorer link
shared host summary/header block when the host surface already renders one
hostRuntime.shellBoundaryRule: Implement only the Vault-specific business UI below the host shell/frame. Do not rebuild token header, breadcrumb, close control, or duplicate a host-provided top summary/hero block inside Component.tsx.
hostRuntime.vaultBannerRule: Do not default to VaultBanner. Use a component-owned summary/banner only when the target host surface truly lacks a standard shared summary block.
hostRuntime.templatePreviewCapability: The right-side preview panel in this template provides a token phase self-test API that can override the local preview host context used by context.host.marketPhase. The panel exposes Real to restore the live host phase and internal-market / dex-listed as explicit local overrides; unknown remains a runtime value rather than a primary tab. On supported preview chains, chainId + tokenAddress is the default source of truth: the preview host uses the public SDK chain-read path to read Portal token state and, when available, matching helper/VaultPortal data. If the live factory or Vault target does not match manifest.match.bindings for the active chain, preview marks the token unavailable and does not render the Vault component. Use marketPhase, isListed, status, or tokenStatusCode only when lifecycle override is intentional.
hostRuntime.marketPhaseRule: The template preview host and production Flap host derive marketPhase from Portal token state: existing tokens with tokenInfo.status < 2 are internal-market; existing tokens with tokenInfo.status >= 2 are dex-listed; missing token info is unknown. In preview, taxInfo=1 is only a fixture seed when no real chain host data is available or when an explicit override is desired.
hostRuntime.riskStatusRule: Every onboarded Vault UI must read and visibly render the current contract risk status from host Vault/TaxInfo context. The risk status must be placed in the first or second row of the Vault-specific business UI so it is visible in the first viewport. If the risk level is unavailable, render a prominent warning/danger message that risk-status integration is required. Do not hardcode or unconditionally render Low risk / 低风险 labels, badges, summaries, or reassuring copy; a low-risk label may appear only when selected from the host-derived riskLevel === 1 branch. vault:check blocks Component.tsx files that do not reference host riskLevel and a visible UI primitive, blocks risk status placed too low in the UI, and blocks manual low-risk labels that are not derived from host riskLevel.
hostRuntime.wrongNetworkCapability: sdk.wallet exposes isWrongNetwork, requiredChainId, requiredChainLabel, canSwitchChain, isSwitchingChain, and switchChain() so write-capable Vault UIs can render a switch-network state before attempting writes.

vaultPackage.folderNameRule: The Vault folder name is the source folder and preview route. User-facing docs and outputs should call it folder name. Use 3-64 characters of lowercase kebab-case: /^[a-z0-9]+(-[a-z0-9]+)*$/.
vaultPackage.previewRegistrationRule: Local preview uses src/vaults/index.ts as a static module map. vault:scaffold registers the folder automatically. If the four files already exist, run yarn vault:register {folder-name}; do not hand-edit registration unless the script cannot parse the index file.

allowedImports.sharedRuntimeOnlyRule: Do not introduce any additional SDK package or SDK-like runtime wrapper. Vault source may use only the shared @/src/sdk and @/src/ui surfaces plus the explicit default allowlist.

manifest.artifactIdRule: artifactId is the unique artifact identity and must match vaultui_<folder-name>_<26-char ULID>. It is validated against the Vault folder name and must be unique across src/vaults/*/manifest.json.
manifest.bindingsRule: match.bindings is a non-empty array of unique runtime targets. Each entry must declare chainId plus either a non-zero factoryAddress for factory-scoped UI, exactly one non-zero vaultAddresses entry for Vault-scoped UI without a factory, or one or more non-zero tokenAddresses entries for token-scoped UI without a factory. A zero factoryAddress is invalid; omit factoryAddress for no-factory mode. Preview/runtime should prefer exact chainId + factoryAddress, chainId + vaultAddress (+ optional tokenAddress), or chainId + tokenAddress matches, treat partial hints as valid only when unambiguous, and use the first binding as a preview default only when the route provides no runtime hints.
manifest.tokenAddressRule: Do not declare global tokenAddresses, restrictTokenAddresses, or caPolicy in manifest.json. If a token CA list is needed, declare it only as match.bindings[].tokenAddresses. In no-factory mode tokenAddresses may be the binding target and may contain multiple token addresses.
manifest.vaultAddressRule: Declare vaultAddresses inside a match.bindings entry. It is optional for factory-scoped and token-scoped bindings, but if provided without factoryAddress it must contain exactly one non-zero Vault address.
manifest.externalContractRule: Declare fixed non-token/non-vault/non-factory contract targets inside match.bindings[].externalContracts with {address, label}. vault:check blocks contract interactions with fixed addresses outside runtime context, factoryAddress, tokenAddresses, vaultAddresses, or externalContracts.
manifest.localeRule: manifest.i18n entries must be locale strings with at least two characters. Only locales declared by manifest.i18n are required and compared.
manifest.oracleRule: Do not declare oracle config in manifest.json. sdk.readOracle usage is reported by vault:check for Flap review.
manifest.endpointRule: Avoid endpoints. Non-oracle fetch targets must be static absolute HTTPS URLs without username/password credentials and declared in manifest.endpoints as a single URL string or an array of URL strings for review. Dynamic, relative, HTTP, credentialed, undeclared, aliased, destructured, or computed browser-global fetch targets are blocked.
manifest.externalFrameRule: Avoid external frames. If a display-only market chart is unavoidable, declare at most one entry in manifest.externalFrames with id, provider, src, and title. Providers are limited to tradingview, dexscreener, and coingecko-terminal. src must be a complete static HTTPS provider URL with a fixed non-empty query string, no credentials, and no hash. Component code may render at most one ReviewedFrame from @/src/ui with static string literal frameId, provider, src, and title props that exactly match the manifest declaration. Do not build iframe URLs dynamically or use srcDoc.
manifest.externalFrameReviewOutputRule: vault:check must print the valid external frame src in the manual-review/external-frame warning and in review.externalFrames[] so Workbench can display the exact iframe src value for human review.
manifest.navigationRule: Component-owned navigation should stay on the active chain explorer only. Do not send users to arbitrary websites from a Vault package.
manifest.metadataWhitelistRule: If an NFT metadata base URL or another reviewed non-oracle host must be fetched directly, declare that base URL in manifest.endpoints. Internal Oracle hosts should normally stay behind sdk.readOracle(...) and host/runtime provisioning rather than raw URL literals.

securityBoundaries.contractInteractionRule: Contract reads, writes, event watches, log/filter calls, and gas estimates must stay on context.vaultAddress, context.tokenAddress, context.factoryAddress, runtime payment/quote/dividend tokens, token/NFT addresses derived from Vault reads, or fixed addresses declared under match.bindings[].externalContracts. Do not interact with routers, bridges, aggregators, or unrelated app contracts from a Vault package.
securityBoundaries.linkRule: Hardcoded external navigation is phishing-sensitive. Only chain explorer destinations are allowed directly from component source. Other hosts must go through manifest endpoint review for data fetches, not user-facing navigation.
securityBoundaries.externalFrameBoundaryRule: ReviewedFrame is the only iframe path and may be used at most once per Vault UI. External frames are display-only review candidates and must not connect wallets, drive quotes, route trades, influence risk/settlement, receive postMessage data, or replace SDK/on-chain/oracle reads.
securityBoundaries.workbenchParityRule: The template checker is intentionally a Workbench-intake safety-floor superset: it must block symlinks, CommonJS require, dynamic imports, credentialed endpoints, unsafe fetch targets, browser-global aliasing/destructuring/computed access, direct browser-global member access except safe window timers and reviewed explorer-only window.open, injected wallet providers, wallet RPC request/send/signing/transaction methods, wallet-client signing utilities, direct browser network/media APIs, browser storage/cookie/cache APIs, navigation/history APIs, Worker/ServiceWorker APIs, script injection APIs such as document.write/writeln and document.open/close, raw iframe/srcDoc usage outside ReviewedFrame, cross-context messaging APIs including postMessage listeners and EIP-6963 provider discovery, and browser permission APIs before packaging.

errorOutput.rule: Agents must parse command failures, fix agent.nextActions, and avoid retrying the same command without changes.

checkerWarnings.manual-review/action-stage-gating: Component has a write path but no marketPhase or isActionAvailableForPhase usage. Add explicit internal-market / DEX-listed button gating and visible unavailable-state copy.
checkerWarnings.risk-status/missing-host-risk-state: Component does not visibly render the current contract risk status from host Vault/TaxInfo context. Add riskLevel handling and a prominent unavailable-risk warning.
checkerWarnings.risk-status/not-prominent-placement: Component renders host risk status too low in the Vault business UI. Move it into the first or second row so it is visible in the first viewport.
checkerWarnings.risk-status/manual-low-risk-label: Component renders Low risk / 低风险 copy without selecting it from host riskLevel === 1. Remove the manual label or map the host-derived risk level explicitly.

template-freshness/npm-outdated.fixAction: Update this checkout so package.json version is at least the npm latest @flapsdk/vault-runtime version before running vault:check, build, runtime:package, or vault:package.

template-freshness/npm-fetch-failed.fixAction: Fix npm registry or network access so the latest @flapsdk/vault-runtime version can be verified.

template-freshness/invalid-version.fixAction: Use valid semver versions in package.json and the published @flapsdk/vault-runtime package.

template-freshness/npm-git-head-mismatch.fixAction: Update this source checkout so its git history contains the npm latest @flapsdk/vault-runtime published commit.

template-freshness/git-head-unverified.fixAction: Run from a valid flap-vault-ui-template git checkout so the npm latest published commit can be verified.

template-freshness/package-json-unreadab.fixAction: Run the command from the flap-vault-ui-template repository root with a readable package.json.

package/runtime-git-head-missing.fixAction: Publish the runtime package from git so npm exposes gitHead provenance, then regenerate the source package.

package-verify/missing-runtime-git-head.fixAction: Regenerate the source package with a current yarn vault:package script that records runtimePackageGitHead.

preview-registration/missing-vault-modul.fixAction: Run yarn vault:register {folder-name} to add the local preview mapping to src/vaults/index.ts.

manifest/missing-artifact-id.fixAction: Add artifactId to manifest.json. Format: vaultui_<folder-name>_<26-char ULID>. Use vault:scaffold to generate it automatically.

manifest/invalid-artifact-id.fixAction: artifactId must match vaultui_<folder-name>_<ULID> and the embedded folder-name segment must equal the Vault folder name. Regenerate with vault:scaffold or correct manually.

manifest/duplicate-artifact-id.fixAction: artifactId is already used by another vault in src/vaults/*/manifest.json. Generate a new ULID segment to make it unique.

manifest-binding/duplicate-binding.fixAction: Remove duplicate match.bindings entries with the same runtime target. Merge any reference tokenAddresses or vaultAddresses into one binding entry.

manifest-binding/zero-factory-address.fixAction: Omit factoryAddress for no-factory mode and provide exactly one non-zero vaultAddresses entry or one or more tokenAddresses, or replace it with the real deployed factory contract address for factory mode.

manifest-binding/mixed-binding-target.fixAction: Use factoryAddress for factory-scoped UI, or omit factoryAddress for Vault/token-scoped no-factory UI. Do not include factoryAddress and vaultAddresses in one binding.

risk-status/missing-host-risk-state.fixAction: Read riskLevel from readTaxVaultHostContext(context.host).vaultInfo or .taxInfo.vaultInfo, display it prominently, and show a warning/danger message when the host risk level is unavailable.

risk-status/manual-low-risk-label.fixAction: Remove hardcoded or unconditional Low risk / 低风险 copy. Low-risk labels may appear only in the host-derived riskLevel === 1 render branch.

risk-status/not-prominent-placement.fixAction: Move the host-derived risk status into the first or second row of the Vault-specific business UI so users can see it in the first viewport.

contract-abi/human-readable-requires-par.fixAction: Import parseAbi from viem and wrap human-readable ABI signature arrays with parseAbi([...]), or replace them with full object ABI fragments.

manifest-binding/missing-binding-target.fixAction: Add either a non-zero factoryAddress or exactly one non-zero vaultAddresses entry to the binding.

manifest/ca-policy-not-in-manifest.fixAction: Remove global tokenAddresses, restrictTokenAddresses, and caPolicy fields. If a reference token CA list is needed, declare it only as match.bindings[].tokenAddresses.

manifest/type-field-binding.fixAction: Remove vaultType or vaultTypes from the manifest match block. Registry binding intent must use match.bindings chain/factory or chain/Vault targets only.

manifest/invalid-folder-name.fixAction: Rename the Vault folder to 3-64 characters of lowercase kebab-case matching /^[a-z0-9]+(-[a-z0-9]+)*$/. No spaces, underscores, uppercase, leading/trailing hyphens, or nested folders.

manifest/invalid-external-contract.fixAction: Each match.bindings[].externalContracts entry must include only address and label. Use it only for fixed non-token/non-Vault/non-factory contract targets.

manifest/endpoint-missing-required-field.fixAction: manifest.endpoints must be either one absolute HTTPS URL string without credentials or a non-empty array of those strings. Fix or remove the invalid endpoint declaration.

package/extra-files.fixAction: Remove all files and folders from src/vaults/{folder-name} that are not Component.tsx, manifest.json, VaultABI.ts, or i18n.json.

package/missing-files.fixAction: Add the missing required files. All four are required: Component.tsx, manifest.json, VaultABI.ts, and i18n.json.

import/forbidden-relative.fixAction: Remove the local relative import. Only ./VaultABI is allowed. Replace with @/src/sdk or @/src/ui for shared surfaces.

import/dynamic-import.fixAction: Remove dynamic import(). Only static imports are allowed inside Vault source.

import/forbidden-package.fixAction: Remove the forbidden dependency. Allowed packages: react, viem, decimal.js, lucide-react, @/src/sdk, @/src/ui, ./VaultABI.

import/external-sdk-package.fixAction: Remove the extra SDK package. Vault source may use only the shared @/src/sdk and @/src/ui runtime surfaces.

security/direct-wallet-access.fixAction: Remove window.ethereum, window.solana, web3/currentProvider, EIP-6963 provider discovery, viem wallet-client/account signing utilities, and raw provider request/send/signing/transaction methods. Use sdk.wallet, sdk.writeContract, and sdk.simulateContract from @/src/sdk.

security/eval.fixAction: Remove eval(), string-based timer callbacks, Function constructor, and constructor-constructor escapes. Runtime code execution is not allowed inside Vault source.

security/iframe-or-script-injection.fixAction: Remove raw iframe elements and script injection. If a display-only TradingView, DexScreener, or CoinGecko Terminal chart is unavoidable, use manifest.externalFrames plus ReviewedFrame with a static src after review.

frame-policy/invalid-frames.fixAction: Set manifest.externalFrames to a non-empty array of reviewed frame declarations, or remove it.

frame-policy/invalid-frame-declaration.fixAction: Each manifest.externalFrames entry must include only id, provider, src, and title. src must be a complete static HTTPS provider URL with a fixed query string.

frame-policy/unsupported-provider.fixAction: Use provider tradingview, dexscreener, or coingecko-terminal only.

frame-policy/unsupported-origin.fixAction: Use only the exact reviewed provider origins for TradingView, DexScreener, or CoinGecko Terminal.

frame-policy/dynamic-frame-src.fixAction: Pass a complete static string literal src to ReviewedFrame. Do not compose provider, path, or query params from runtime data.

frame-policy/undeclared-frame-src.fixAction: Declare the exact ReviewedFrame src in manifest.externalFrames with the same frameId and provider, or remove the frame.

frame-policy/too-many-reviewed-frames.fixAction: Keep at most one manifest.externalFrames entry and one ReviewedFrame usage per Vault UI.

manual-review/external-frame.fixAction: The external frame requires Flap review before production publish. Confirm the frame is display-only and cannot affect quotes, risk, settlement, or transactions.

security/undeclared-url.fixAction: Declare data fetch URLs in manifest.endpoints, declare the reviewed display-only chart frame in manifest.externalFrames, or remove the URL and use sdk.readContract or sdk.readOracle instead.

security/host-relative-fetch.fixAction: Remove fetch('/api/...') and all host-relative calls. They can accidentally target private Flap app routes. Use sdk.readOracle for oracle data or declare a full HTTPS endpoint in manifest.endpoints.

security/non-https-resource.fixAction: Remove non-HTTPS, credentialed, IPFS, Arweave, WebSocket, browser-global, browser-storage, browser-navigation, browser-worker, browser-permission, or embedded data URL resources. Only declared credential-free HTTPS endpoints are allowed.

security/hardcoded-address.fixAction: Replace hardcoded EVM addresses with runtime context values: context.vaultAddress, context.tokenAddress, context.factoryAddress, or other context fields.

security/remote-media.fixAction: Remove remote image URLs from Vault source. Token media must come from context.tokenImageUrl, which the host/preview shell loads from Flap-controlled metadata.

security/unapproved-navigation.fixAction: Remove the off-site navigation. Only current-chain explorer links are allowed directly from component source; reviewed metadata/oracle exceptions must be allowlisted outside the public package.

contract/missing-label.fixAction: Add a human-readable contract label such as vault, token, or nft so review and static checks can classify the target.

contract/disallowed-label.fixAction: Limit contract labels to vault/token/nft-related targets. Do not call routers, bridges, aggregators, or unrelated app contracts from a Vault package.

contract/disallowed-address-source.fixAction: Keep contract targets on context.vaultAddress, context.tokenAddress, context.factoryAddress, token/NFT-related runtime addresses, or declared externalContracts only.

contract/undeclared-address.fixAction: Declare intentional fixed non-token/non-Vault/non-factory contract targets under match.bindings[].externalContracts, or replace the target with a runtime context address.

i18n/missing-locale.fixAction: Add the missing locale to i18n.json. All locales declared in manifest.i18n must have a corresponding object in i18n.json.

i18n/missing-key.fixAction: Add the missing i18n key to the locale in i18n.json. All keys must be present in every locale declared by manifest.i18n.

abi/erc20-in-vault-abi.fixAction: Remove standard ERC20 ABI fragments from VaultABI.ts and replace with erc20Abi or standardErc20Abi imported from @/src/sdk.

manual-review/action-stage-gating.fixAction: Add explicit marketPhase and isActionAvailableForPhase usage. Gate write actions by internal-market or dex-listed stage. Show unavailable buttons with clear copy instead of hiding them.

manual-review/declared-endpoint.fixAction: The declared endpoint requires Flap review before production publish. Ensure the endpoint is truly necessary. Declaration does not guarantee approval.

manual-review/oracle-usage.fixAction: sdk.readOracle usage detected. The oracle id will be submitted to Flap Artifact Workbench for review and provisioning. No change to manifest.json is needed.

requiredInputs.description: All inputs an agent must collect before generating a new Vault UI. See docs/agent-intake-template.md for the structured Q&A conversation guide.

folderName.description: Vault source folder and local preview route. Lowercase kebab-case only. Example: my-vault.

name.description: Human-readable UI name shown in the Flap Artifact Workbench. Example: My Vault UI.

chainId.description: EVM chain ID. Example: 56 for BNB Chain mainnet, 97 for testnet.

factoryAddress.description: Non-zero factory contract address on this chain for factory-scoped UI.

vaultAddresses.description: Vault address list for this binding. If provided without factoryAddress, it must contain exactly one address.

tokenAddresses.description: Optional token CA list for this binding. In no-factory mode this may be the binding target and may contain multiple token addresses.

bindings.description: Explicit binding targets. Use chainId + factoryAddress for factory-scoped UI, chainId + exactly one vaultAddresses entry for Vault-scoped no-factory UI, or chainId + tokenAddresses for token-scoped no-factory UI.

vaultAddresses.description: Vault address list. In manifest.json, vaultAddresses is nested inside each match.bindings entry. It is required with exactly one address when factoryAddress is omitted.

locales.description: Supported locale list. Each locale must be at least two characters. vault:check validates exactly these locales. Example: [en, zh] or [zh].

vaultAbi.description: Minimal Vault ABI fragments for the component. Exclude standard ERC20; use erc20Abi from @/src/sdk for balanceOf, allowance, approve, decimals, symbol, transfer, transferFrom.

actionAvailabilityStage.description: When component write actions are available relative to the token's market phase. Must be decided before implementing Component.tsx.

primaryReads.description: Vault and token reads the UI displays.

primaryWrites.description: Write methods the user can invoke.

approvalSpender.description: Spender address for ERC20 approve. Usually context.vaultAddress.

nativeValue.description: Which write sends native token and how the amount is computed. Omit if none.

refetchPoints.description: Events after which data should reload. Example: after approve, after deposit.

emptyStates.description: States the UI must handle explicitly. Example: no wallet, no position, paused.

riskPosture.description: How this Vault should be presented to users.

uiWorkflow.description: Full workflow decisions. All sub-fields must be answered before implementation begins.

previewAddresses.description: Real addresses for local preview. Recommended. Not packaged into the source zip.

purpose.description: Why the display-only frame is needed instead of SDK/on-chain/oracle data.

externalFrames.description: Optional reviewed display-only chart embeds. Each src must be the complete static provider URL with fixed query params.

doneReport.description: Structured handoff summary agents must produce after vault:verify-package succeeds. Present to the user before closing the task.
doneReport.formatDefinition: Markdown with stable top-level labels in this order: Summary, Package, Validation, Preview, Open Items. Use short key: value lines under each label. Do not emit raw JSON unless the user asks for machine-only output.

fields.name: folderName
fields.description: Vault folder name used as source directory and preview route.

fields.name: sourcePackagePath
fields.description: Relative path to the generated zip, from vault:package output sourcePackagePath field.

fields.name: sha256
fields.description: SHA-256 of the generated zip, from vault:package output.

fields.name: runtimePackageGitHead
fields.description: npm latest @flapsdk/vault-runtime gitHead recorded by the source package marker.

fields.name: checkSummary
fields.description: blocking and warning counts from the final vault:check run.

fields.name: actionAvailabilityStage
fields.description: Stage decision made for this Vault: internal-market, dex-listed, both, or read-only.

fields.name: selectedPatterns
fields.description: One or more pattern names from docs/ui-pattern-snippets.md coverage matrix.

fields.name: locales
fields.description: Locales declared in manifest.i18n and validated by vault:check.

fields.name: openItems
fields.description: Unresolved assumptions, missing contract inputs, oracle IDs pending Flap provisioning, endpoint declarations pending Flap review, or external frame declarations pending Flap review.

openItemsSources: Any vault:check issue with ruleId manual-review/oracle-usage; include the oracle id and expected response assumption.
Any vault:check warning with ruleId manual-review/external-endpoint; include each endpoint URL, purpose, and why SDK/on-chain reads are insufficient.
Any vault:check warning with ruleId manual-review/external-frame; include the frame id, provider, src, and why SDK/on-chain/oracle reads are insufficient.
Any skipped preview requirement, especially marketPhase=internal-market, marketPhase=dex-listed, or wrong-network state for write-capable flows.
Any missing contract input, unresolved ABI assumption, missing preview address, or user-confirmation dependency.
Any Workbench review dependency outside this template, including endpoint approval, external frame approval, oracle provisioning, registry binding, or runtime publish approval.
doneReport.rule: Agents must produce this report after vault:verify-package succeeds. If openItems is non-empty, list each item explicitly so the user knows what still requires Flap review or manual input. If openItems is empty, state openItems: none.

workflow.description: Read agent-contract.json, the tool-specific entry point, docs/ai-agent.md, docs/agent-entrypoints.md, docs/ui-pattern-snippets.md, and the generator skill before editing.
Use scaffold-first or manifest-first. Scaffold-first runs yarn vault:scaffold {folder-name} ...; manifest-first writes the four Vault files, then runs yarn vault:register {folder-name}.
Select a pattern from docs/ui-pattern-snippets.md, then edit only Component.tsx, manifest.json, VaultABI.ts, and i18n.json inside src/vaults/{folder-name}.
Run yarn dev and open /{folder-name}. If it 404s, run yarn vault:check {folder-name} and fix preview-registration/missing-vault-module.
Run yarn vault:check {folder-name}; fix all blocking issues; then run yarn vault:package {folder-name} and yarn vault:verify-package dist/{folder-name}.zip.

sourcePackage.acceptanceRule: Flap Artifact Workbench should reject manually assembled zips, zips missing the marker, zips with duplicate entries, zips with mismatched hashes, unexpected entries, missing runtimePackageGitHead provenance, and zips with an unsupported package kind or format version.

doneCriteria: folder name is registered in src/vaults/index.ts
manifest.artifactId exists, matches vaultui_<folder-name>_<ULID>, and is unique
yarn vault:check {folder-name} reports zero blocking issues
zip contains flap-vault-package.json generated by yarn vault:package with runtimePackageGitHead provenance
stage-gated actions are previewed with marketPhase=internal-market and marketPhase=dex-listed
current contract risk status is displayed from host riskLevel, with a prominent missing-risk warning state
contract risk status appears in the first or second row of the Vault-specific business UI
no Low risk / 低风险 label or reassuring low-risk copy appears unless selected from host riskLevel === 1
write-capable flows preview a wrong-network state and render a switch-network path instead of sending writes on the wrong chain
Vault folder contains only the four allowed files
Component.tsx follows a public-safe pattern from docs/ui-pattern-snippets.md
action availability stage is decided and reflected in visible enabled/disabled states
all Component.tsx user-facing copy is in i18n.json
contract reads and writes use runtime context addresses
